{"step_by_step_thinking": "Perampanel is a selective noncompetitive antagonist of AMPA glutamate receptors and is primarily used for the treatment of seizures. The retrieved documents do not provide any evidence that perampanel is approved or studied for use in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS). In fact, the documents specifically mention that perampanel has shown promise in juvenile-onset DRPLA (Dentatorubral Pallidoluysian Atrophy) and in the treatment of myoclonus, but not in ALS. For ALS, the only approved medications are riluzole, edaravone, and sodium phenylbutyrate, all of which have limited efficacy. There is no mention of perampanel being used, studied, or recommended in ALS in any of the documents. Therefore, based on the available evidence, perampanel should not be used for ALS.", "answer_choice": "B"}
